description: Cached structured outputs for the eight Crucible AI agents. Used when USE_CACHED_AI=true or when a live call fails schema validation. Keys match prompt names in lib/ai/prompts/.

hypothesis.title: Lossless follow-up for agencies
hypothesis.productSummary: Crucible reads call notes and drafts personalized second-touch follow-ups so agency founders stop losing warm prospects between discovery calls and close.
hypothesis.icpGuess: 5-25 person agencies (marketing, design, ops) where the founder runs discovery calls and is the bottleneck on follow-ups.
hypothesis.likelyBuyer: Agency founder or COO with budget authority and felt pain over lost warm leads.
hypothesis.likelyUser: Founder, head of sales, or studio manager who runs discovery calls and is responsible for follow-up.
hypothesis.champion: Studio manager or RevOps lead who feels the lost-revenue pain weekly.
hypothesis.painClaim: Warm prospects go cold between the first call and the second touch because the founder is the bottleneck.
hypothesis.proofPoint: Design partners report recovering 1-3 warm deals per month after wiring it into their existing call notes workflow.
messageAngles: pipeline recovery framing (recover warm deals already in motion)
riskyAssumptions: Agency owners frame the pain as missed revenue rather than time savings.
Buyers will accept a draft-first workflow over full automation.
Tool-fatigued operators care more about implementation cost than price.

archetypes.name: Overworked Agency Owner
archetypes.description: Runs discovery calls personally and is the bottleneck on follow-up. Loses warm deals to slow second touches.
archetypes.currentWorkflow: Calls in calendar, notes in Notion or Google Docs, follow-ups manually drafted in Gmail when remembered.
likelyReplyPatterns: asks for a demo or a fit check

archetypes.name: Skeptical Solo Consultant
archetypes.segment: Solo or 1-2 person outbound / strategy consultancy
archetypes.description: Treats outbound as a craft. Distrusts AI tools. Books from referrals and signature.
archetypes.currentWorkflow: Hand-written follow-ups, no automation, deliberate cadence.

archetypes.name: Ops-Minded Studio Manager
archetypes.description: Optimizes the founder's day. Allergic to bloat. Wants clean workflow integration.
archetypes.currentWorkflow: Owns CRM hygiene and follow-up reminders for the founder. Has reduced tool count this year.
archetypes.bestAngle: replaces a workflow you already have

archetypes.name: Growth-Focused Founder
archetypes.description: Cares about pipeline metrics and revenue recovery. Buys when ROI is legible.
archetypes.currentWorkflow: HubSpot or similar CRM, sequenced follow-ups, but second-touch personalization is weak.
archetypes.bestAngle: pipeline recovery (warm deals already in motion)

archetypes.name: Tool-Fatigued Operator
archetypes.description: Has cancelled multiple SaaS subscriptions this year. Refuses new tools until current ones earn their keep.
archetypes.currentWorkflow: Slimmed-down stack; deliberate freeze on adding software.

archetypes.name: Budget-Sensitive Operator
archetypes.description: Strong cost discipline. Pushes back on per-seat pricing. Will buy if ROI is concrete.
archetypes.currentWorkflow: Lean stack. CFO has tightened software budgets.

archetypes.name: Trust-First Buyer
archetypes.description: Wants human-in-the-loop. Allergic to anything that auto-sends. Won't try a tool without a clear approval workflow.
archetypes.currentWorkflow: Manual everything. Considers automation only if approval is mandatory.

archetypes.name: Wrong-Person Gatekeeper
archetypes.segment: EAs / coordinators at larger agencies, 20-50 people
archetypes.description: Forwards or filters vendor outreach. Will redirect if relevance is obvious.
archetypes.currentWorkflow: Manages the founder's inbox. Triage and forward.
dislikedPhrases: thought you'd be the right person

archetypes.name: Competitor-Locked Buyer
archetypes.description: Already paying for a competing solution. Under contract. Open to evaluating only on renewal.
archetypes.currentWorkflow: Established outbound stack with multi-month contract.

archetypes.name: Interested-But-Later Buyer
archetypes.segment: Growth or RevOps at agency, 15-30 people
archetypes.role: Director of Marketing or Marketing Ops
archetypes.description: Plans on quarterly cycles. Says yes-in-principle but parks until next planning window.
archetypes.currentWorkflow: Quarterly OKRs; budget freezes between cycles.
archetypes.bestAngle: low-cost pilot now, decide at next planning

matchedSignals: Substack post distrusting AI sales tools
Slack post asking about systemizing follow-ups
OKR doc names follow-up as #1 leak
LinkedIn post on AI tools losing him
no new tools without retiring one
public praise of current outbound stack
AMA quote on doing more with less
thread on automation that earns trust slowly
explicit tool freeze in all-hands deck
solo seller, too busy to follow up
recap email saying revisit in 6-8 weeks

phrasesToUse: if you're not the right person
phrasesToAvoid: I think you're the right person

emails.hypothesis: Agency owners respond to missed-revenue framing more than AI automation framing.
emails.subject: the warm deals you lost last month
emails.body: Maya - saw your post about losing two warm deals on the second touch. That's the exact gap we built for. Crucible reads your call notes and drafts the second follow-up; you approve before anything sends. No new dashboard, just a draft in your inbox. Open to a 15-min fit check next week to see if it would have caught those two? - happy to walk away if it's not a fit.
emails.followUp1: Maya - bumping in case the first slipped. One question: which deal felt most preventable? That's usually the best test case for a fit check.
emails.followUp2: Last note from me - if the second follow-up isn't your bottleneck I'll stop. If it is, the fit check is 15 minutes and you'll know in 5.
emails.complianceFooter: If this isn't a fit, reply 'no' and I won't follow up.

emails.hypothesis: Tool-fatigued operators object on price first; lead with transparent pricing.
emails.subject: transparent pricing for your follow-up workflow
emails.body: Marcus - read your post on AI tools losing you. We charge a flat per-workspace fee, no per-seat creep, and we draft only - you approve before anything sends. No new dashboard, plugs into your existing call notes. Open to a 15-min fit check to see if the price math works for Verdant?
emails.followUp1: Marcus - quick bump. The flat-rate piece tends to be the dealbreaker in this category, so I want to make sure that part is clear before we talk.
emails.followUp2: Closing the loop - if pricing isn't the issue I'd love to hear what is. Either way, last note from me.
emails.complianceFooter: If this isn't a fit, reply 'no' and I won't follow up.
riskWarnings: pricing-first framing may miss the real objection

emails.hypothesis: Tool-fatigued operators object on price first; lead with transparent pricing.
emails.subject: flat pricing, no per-seat creep
emails.body: Aisling - saw the thread on tool graveyards. Crucible is a flat per-workspace fee, drafts only, you approve everything before send. No new dashboard. Worth a 15-min fit check to see if the price math holds up against your current stack?
emails.followUp1: Aisling - bumping once. The flat-rate detail is the part most ops folks want clear up front.
emails.followUp2: Last bump from me. If the pricing isn't the blocker I'd love to know what is.
emails.complianceFooter: If this isn't a fit, reply 'no' and I won't follow up.
riskWarnings: pricing-first framing may miss the real objection

emails.hypothesis: Tool-fatigued operators object on price first; lead with transparent pricing.
emails.subject: price math for Signalboost
emails.body: Tomasz - 'tools that didn't stick' was uncomfortably real. We're a flat per-workspace fee with drafts-only and human approval. No new dashboard, plugs into existing call notes. Worth 15 minutes to see if the unit economics work for Signalboost?
emails.followUp1: Tomasz - quick bump. Specifically curious whether the flat-rate vs per-seat structure changes the calc for you.
emails.followUp2: Last note. If price isn't the issue I want to know what is.
emails.complianceFooter: If this isn't a fit, reply 'no' and I won't follow up.
riskWarnings: pricing-first framing may miss the real objection

emails.hypothesis: Agency owners respond to missed-revenue framing more than AI automation framing.
emails.subject: the second follow-up problem
emails.body: Sofia - heard the podcast clip about the second follow-up being your weak spot. We built Crucible for exactly that: it reads your call notes and drafts the second touch; you approve before anything sends. Open to a 15-min fit check?
emails.followUp1: Sofia - bumping. Which warm lead from the last month felt most preventable? That's usually the best fit-check input.
emails.followUp2: Last bump from me - if the second follow-up isn't your bottleneck I'll stop.
emails.complianceFooter: If this isn't a fit, reply 'no' and I won't follow up.

emails.hypothesis: Gatekeepers redirect cleanly when the ask is obvious and easy to forward.
emails.subject: right person at Frontline for follow-up tooling?
emails.body: Hannah - if you're not the right person on outbound follow-up workflow at Frontline, who would you point me to? Two-line context: we draft the second follow-up from existing call notes; the founder approves before anything sends. Happy to wait for the redirect.
emails.followUp1: Hannah - bumping; just need a name or 'not us' and I'll stop.
emails.followUp2: Last note - assuming this isn't a fit unless you redirect me, no need to reply.
emails.complianceFooter: If this isn't a fit, reply 'no' and I won't follow up.

emails.hypothesis: Quarterly buyers commit to pilots if scoped small and tied to their planning cycle.
emails.subject: small first-cohort pilot at Parallel Grow
emails.body: Camille - given Q3 planning is around the corner, would a small first-cohort pilot (one campaign, 25 prospects) make sense before the planning meeting? Drafts only, you approve everything. No commitment past the cohort.
emails.followUp1: Camille - bumping. If timing is the only blocker I can pre-stage the pilot for the first week of August.
emails.followUp2: Last note - if the planning window is locked I'll re-ping in early August.
emails.complianceFooter: If this isn't a fit, reply 'no' and I won't follow up.

reply_marcus_rendon.mismatchReason: Predicted pricing; actual blocker is implementation time.

volunteeredInfo: open if it replaces a workflow
reply_aisling_murphy.mismatchReason: Predicted pricing; actual is tool/setup freeze tied to implementation overhead.

reply_tomasz_kaminski.mismatchReason: Predicted pricing; prospect explicitly says price isn't it; setup time is.

calibrationAgent.summary: Tool-Fatigued Operator predictions miss systematically: predicted pricing, observed implementation/setup time. Three of three sent emails to this archetype show the same mismatch. Update the archetype.

updates.reason: Three of three predictions wrong; the systemic miss is implementation/setup time, not price.

added: I do not have time to set up another tool.
Responds better to '15-min fit check' than 'book a demo.'

nextCohort.summary: Cohort 2 leads with implementation-light framing for the Tool-Fatigued Operator segment and pulls back from pricing-first language. Doubles down on missed-revenue framing for Overworked Agency Owners. Pauses the Solo Consultant segment.
changesFromPreviousCohort: Tool-Fatigued Operator emails reframed from 'value beats price' to 'one-evening setup, drafts only.'
Replaced 'book a demo' with '15-min fit check' globally.
Removed 'platform' and 'automation suite' from outbound vocabulary.

newEmailTemplates.subject: 30-minute install, drafts only
newEmailTemplates.body: Never lose a warm lead because you forgot the second follow-up - we draft it, you approve it. No new dashboard, no auto-send, 30-minute install on top of your existing call notes. Worth a 15-min fit check?
newEmailTemplates.hypothesis: Tool-fatigued operators respond to implementation-light framing rather than pricing.

nextCohort.killCriterion: Pause the Tool-Fatigued Operator framing again if Cohort 2 prediction accuracy for that archetype stays below 0.5.
nextCohort.successMetric: Cohort 2 reply rate for Tool-Fatigued Operator >= 0.4 with prediction accuracy >= 0.65.
nextCohort.afterEmail: Never lose a warm lead because you forgot the second follow-up - we draft it, you approve it.
